title: T-018 — API: Manufacturing operation allergen additions CRUD
prompt:
# T-018 — API: Manufacturing operation allergen additions CRUD

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §10.4, §6.5, §10.7
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
CRUD on manufacturing_operation_allergen_additions table with reference_table FK validation.

Used by allergen cascade rule when computing FG allergens (UNION RM allergens via BOM components + manufacturing operation allergens added). Org-scoped with RLS.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- app/api/technical/manufacturing-operations/allergens/route.ts [create]
- lib/technical/allergens/manufacturing-op.ts [create]
- tests/api/manufacturing-op-allergens.test.ts [create]

## Out of scope
- Cascade rule (T-024)
- UI (TEC-042)

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given POST with manufacturing_operation_name not in reference_table, when validated, then 422 V-TEC-63.
2. Given valid POST, when GET runs, then row appears for that manufacturing_operation_name.
3. Given DELETE, when audit_log queried, then action='manufacturing_op.allergen.delete' exists.

## Test strategy
- RED tests for FK validation + audit.
- Run: pnpm vitest run tests/api/manufacturing-op-allergens.test.ts

## Risk red lines
- Do not allow ad-hoc manufacturing_operation_name values — must reference Reference table.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
